name: domain-quantum-synthesis
description: Use web_search to search for 'quantum computing timeline practical advantage forecast 2030 outlook'. Results overlap with earlier quantum searches. Synthesize: when will quantum computers achieve practical advantage? Key milestones, remaining barriers, and realistic timeline. Keep response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer: Practical quantum advantage is expected by 2028-2030, with narrow domain-specific advantages already emerging (IonQ/Ansys demonstrated a 12% speedup in 2025). Key milestones include IBM's 200 logical qubit Starling system by 2029, IonQ targeting 80,000 logical qubits by 2030, and Quantinuum's universal fault-tolerant Apollo system by 2030. Primary barriers remain decoherence, error correction overhead requiring millions of physical qubits, and competition from advancing classical AI. The market is projected to reach $20 billion by 2030, with BCG forecasting broad quantum advantage in the 2030-2040 window and full fault tolerance after 2040. Forrester warns Q-day (breaking RSA-2048) could arrive by 2030, making post-quantum cryptography migration urgent now.
key_facts: Forrester projects practical quantum computing and Q-day (breaking RSA-2048) are both likely by 2030
BCG segments the timeline: NISQ until 2030, broad quantum advantage 2030-2040, full fault tolerance after 2040
IBM targets 200 logical qubits by 2029; IonQ targets 80,000 logical qubits by 2030
IonQ and Ansys demonstrated a narrow practical quantum advantage (12% speedup) in March 2025
Competition from classical AI and algorithmic optimization remains a risk to quantum timelines
